steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main idea of the poem. The speaker describes the calm of a port at evening and then — marked by “Then, and then only, have I thought” — reflects that old age could be sweet and quiet after a “windy youth.” The correct answer must capture both the triggering scene (evening port tranquility) and the thought it prompts (appreciation of old age's stillness).

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'The tranquility of a port in the evening can incline a person to appreciate the stillness of old age.': matches exactly: the calm scene prompts the speaker to welcome old age as “sweet,” “quiet,” and “gracious.” ✓
  • Choice B — 'The difficulty of bringing a ship into port is apt training for dealing with the types of struggles encountered in old age.': introduces difficulty in docking, which is absent from the poem. The poem also depicts old age as peaceful rather than full of struggles. ✗
  • Choice C — 'A person who leads a long life that is varied and active may find it difficult to stay in a calm place.': contradicts the poem's tone. The speaker welcomes old age's calm; there is no suggestion of difficulty accepting stillness. ✗
  • Choice D — 'The contrast between the peacefulness of a port at night and its activity during the day reflects the contrast between the calm of old age and the vibrancy of youth.': the poem never describes daytime activity at the port, so a day/night contrast is not established. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
